rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH CONNELLY VS. COLLEEN HOLLAND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Terminating Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 24, 2014, Line 7, DEFENDANT COLLEEN HOLLAND's Motion For Terminating Sanctions. The court grants Ms. Holland's motion for a terminating sanction in part pursuant to CCP 128(a)(4). The evidence demonstrates that Mr. Connelly has continued to violate the court's preliminary injunction and contempt orders. Mr. Connelly has not provided sufficient access to Ms. Holland for the VegNews' accounts. (Holland dec., pars. 38, 46, and 58). Ms. Holland also introduces additional evidence of Mr. Connelly's bad faith conduct, including engaging in an illegal surveillance scheme. (Holland dec., pars. 32-35; Penal Code sec. 637.7). The court strikes the declaration of Mr. Connelly because it does not comply with CCP 2015.5 and is not under penalty of perjury. The court issues an evidentiary sanction and rules that Ms. Holland is half (50%) owner of VegNews and that finding is established for all purposes. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: LISA TREJO VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Regarding Plaintiff'S Expert Disclosure; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 24, 2014, Line 2, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO Motion For Protective Order Regarding Plaintiff'S Expert Disclosure; Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted. Sanctions denied. Plaintiff shall serve by hand-delivery, or by email and overnight mail, a list by the close of business on Monday, October 27, containing only the names of those non-retained experts who she intends to have testify at trial. The actual names of all non-retained experts must be given. Simply listing "custodian" is unacceptable. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highmanlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: FRANK CHU VS. ISRAEL GONZALEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Reclassify Action To Court Of Unlimited Jurisdiction; And Motion To Reclassify
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 24, 2014, Line 8, PLAINTIFF FRANK CHU's Motion To Reclassify Action To Court Of Unlimited Jurisdiction. Granted. A motion to reclassify from a limited to an unlimited case must be granted unless it appears to a legal certainty that plaintiff's damages will necessarily be $25,000 or less. (Ytuarte v. Sup. Ct. (2005) 129 Cal.App.4th 266, 279, "the court should reject the plaintiff's effort to reclassify the action as unlimited only when the lack of jurisdiction as an 'unlimited' case is certain and clear.") Plaintiff submits evidence that he underwent neck surgery to alleviate arm pain. Plaintiff may be able to establish through expert testimony that the neck surgery was attributable, at least in part, to the subject car accident. Therefore, it is not a legal certainty that Plaintiff will be unable to recover more than $25,000. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: TERESITA U. DEE, AN INDIVIDUAL VS. R-MAC & ASSOCIATES, INC.A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss And, Alternatively, Stay Action For Forum Non Convenies
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 24, 2014, Line 9, DEFENDANT CLUB ACQUISITION COMPANY, JAMES BUTLER, ROB RIOS, R-MAC PROPERTIES, INC., SAN FELIPE BEACH CLUB S.A. DE C.V., RANCHO EPOCAS DE ORO S.A. DE C.V.'s Motion To Dismiss And, Alternatively, Stay Action For Forum Non Conveniens. The motion to dismiss or stay this action is denied. The mandatory forum selection clauses that defendants rely upon do not apply to this action. In the First Amended Complaint, plaintiff is suing for breach of the Transfer Agreement. The Transfer Agreement is an independent contract and it does not have a forum selection or Mexican choice of law clause. It is true that plaintiff's fraud and concealment causes of action refer to condominium unit 553 and do not directly reference the Transfer Agreement. Plaintiff, however, never executed an agreement regarding that unit with a forum selection or Mexican choice of law clause. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JORDANNE HO-SHUE et al VS. GDL SFO, INC. DBA GOLD DUST LOUNGE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 24, 2014, Line 11, DEFENDANT GDL SFO, INC., DBA GOLD DUST AT THE WHARF's Motion To Compel Arbitration. Denied. The Dispute Resolution Policy agreed to by Plaintiff specifically excludes disputes related to wage and hour issues. (Declaration of Nick Bovis, Ex. A., "However, disputes related to¿wage and hour issues are not arbitral.") The causes of action asserted by Plaintiff are wage and hour claims. Therefore, based on the plain language of the Dispute Resolution Policy, drafted by Defendant, there is not "an agreement to arbitrate the controversy." (CCP sec. 1281.2.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: KAZUKO K ARTUS VS. GRAMERCY TOWERS CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION : A ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 24, 2014, Line 12, PLAINTIFF KAZUKO ARTUS' Motion For Preliminary Injunction. Granted. Defendant Gramercy Towers Condominium Association is enjoined from implementing the result of the July 25, 2014 member election until a trial on the merits is held. Plaintiff has established a likelihood of success. The evidence shows that Defendant included a letter advocating for the passage of the amendment with the ballot, without providing Plaintiff equal opportunity to include literature opposing the amendment, in violation of Civ. Code sec. 5105(a)(1). (Declaration of Kazuko Artus, pars. 25-29, Ex. M.) This unfairly gave an appearance of legitimacy to Defendant's position on the Amendment. (See e.g. Knoll v. Davidson (1974) 12 Cal.3d 335, 352.) The evidence also shows that Defendant used association funds to include the letter with the ballot materials, in violation of Civ. Code sec. 5135(a). (Artus Decl., pars. 25-29, Ex. M.) Plaintiff has established that the balance of harms weighs in her favor. If the injunction is not issued, a director's election would proceed in January implementing a procedure that resulted from an election that did not comply with the Davis-Sterling Act. The injunction merely maintains the status quo until a trial on the merits may be held. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
